questions.question: Define and explain the concept of a stack. Provide an example to illustrate its operations.
questions.hint: Stack is a linear data structure that follows the Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) principle.

questions.question: Discuss the stack operations (push, pop, peek, isEmpty) and explain their importance in programming.
questions.hint: These operations are fundamental in evaluating the expression in Reverse Polish Notation and implementing recursive algorithms.

questions.question: Describe a real-world scenario where a stack data structure can be applied, and explain how it would work.
questions.hint: Example: Evaluating postfix expressions involving operators and operands.
